title: MeshCore Nodes – Interactive Map & Analytics Dashboard (Community Project)

messages.author: nutcracker
messages.text: I’ve been hacking on a local-first analytics dashboard for MeshCore and thought some of you might find it useful / interesting What it does: Interactive map of MeshCore nodes with radius-based filtering Click the map to set a centre point → everything recalculates live Search by node name or public key Built-in analytics: Daily node additions (stacked by type) Cumulative growth Moving averages Node type distribution Distance from centre Last-seen / freshness buckets Results table synced with the map Hover a row → the node highlights on the map Smart client-side caching Pulls all nodes once No backend hits for searches / filters Auto-refreshes every 5 minutes (or manually) Why I built it: To explore regional growth, density, and activity in the MeshCore network To reduce load on the central MeshCore backend while still enabling rich analysis To experiment with what’s possible using just the /nodes snapshot data Repo (code + screenshots + install instructions): https://github.com/nutkracker/Meshcore-Nodes/ Important: This is not an official MeshCore project — just a community-built explorer. Huge thanks to the MeshCore team for all the work behind the platform https://meshcore.co.uk Feedback, ideas, and feature requests are very welcome — especially around future analytics or visualisations (edited)
messages.content_html:
I’ve been hacking on a local-first analytics dashboard for MeshCore and thought some of you might find it useful / interesting What it does:
  • Interactive map of MeshCore nodes with radius-based filtering
  • Click the map to set a centre point → everything recalculates live
  • Search by node name or public key
  • Built-in analytics:
    • Daily node additions (stacked by type)
    • Cumulative growth
    • Moving averages
    • Node type distribution
    • Distance from centre
    • Last-seen / freshness buckets
    Results table synced with the map
  • Hover a row → the node highlights on the map Smart client-side caching
  • Pulls all nodes once
  • No backend hits for searches / filters
  • Auto-refreshes every 5 minutes (or manually) Why I built it:
  • To explore regional growth, density, and activity in the MeshCore network
  • To reduce load on the central MeshCore backend while still enabling rich analysis
  • To experiment with what’s possible using just the /nodes snapshot data Repo (code + screenshots + install instructions):
https://github.com/nutkracker/Meshcore-Nodes/ Important: This is not an official MeshCore project — just a community-built explorer. Huge thanks to the MeshCore team for all the work behind the platform https://meshcore.co.uk Feedback, ideas, and feature requests are very welcome — especially around future analytics or visualisations (edited)
embeds_html:
GitHub - nutkracker/Meshcore-Nodes: An interactive, map-driven anal...
An interactive, map-driven analytics dashboard for exploring MeshCore nodes, growth, geography, and activity—built to minimise backend load through smart client-side caching. - nutkracker/Meshcore-...

messages.text: it uses the MeshCore API (essentially the same calls that the Meshcore MAP is using today)

embeds_html:
MeshCore Packet Analyzer
Hi @hejagithub There’s no public API that I intend others to use. There’s not much stopping someone from using these APIs by scraping the data (e.g., making API requests like the UI does), but this would be unauthorized. Unauthorized use of the APIs or websocket feed is not recommended, even if technically not difficult. In the near ...
